verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Cerro After work (16:30 start)
verdict.reason: Cerro is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

conversationalSummary.text: Quick note for TODAY: after work from around 16:30, Cerro is probably the best bet, but it looks a bit too soft for what you’d usually want on a bodyboard. Wind is mixed and the tide’s rising, so it may shape up in the bay, yet it can end up less defined than the main peaks and rocks can sneak close. Do a Surfline and MEO Beachcam check before you leave—if it doesn’t look clearly better than expected, I’d skip.

best.bestWind: ideal SSW, usable S/SW/SSE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Cerro looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: not captured, energy not captured, 9 s period, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable.  Watch: Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size.
best.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size

wetsuit.reason: Warm water, warm air, and light wind make the 3/2 reasonable.

ranked.explanation: Baleal looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 29 kJ, 9 s period, lully 19/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the Baleal sandbanks; Baleal looks below your bodyboard fun size; Baleal may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is part of the picture, but not the deciding factor.
